response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates 15+ years of strong software development experience, they are missing a critical required qualification: native-level Japanese proficiency. Additionally, their career has focused on web/backend platforms (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the embedded networking security expertise required. No demonstrable experience with required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), networking protocols (NETCONF, SNMP), or firmware-level switch product development.
strengths: 15+ years of software development across multiple domains and languages, far exceeding the 3+ year requirement
C/C++ experience with embedded systems context (National Instruments LabWindows, robotic arms, device orchestration tools)
Demonstrated Linux proficiency and systems-level thinking through kernel exposure, infrastructure work, and DevOps experience
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned—a stated required qualification for the role
C programming expertise appears dated; primary recent languages are TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not the C-based UNIX development required
No demonstrated background in embedded networking security, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), network management tools (NETCONF, SNMP), or LAN-switch product development

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded networking security expertise
Career moved away from C/embedded systems focus
